case_title: FINISAR CORPORATION VS. JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER FINISAR CORPORATION'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. PETITIONER PROVIDED A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE & IN ADDITION, RESPONDENT FILED A "RESPONSE" (OPPOSITION). PETITIONER ALSO INDICATED IT FILED AN "OPPOSITION" TO RESPONDENT'S RESPONSE. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO 10/20/11 AT 9:30AM. PARTIES HAVE PROVIDED COURTESY COPIES. RESPONDENT HAS ALSO INDICATED THAT THEY HAVE SET A MOTION TO STAY FOR 10/20/11 AS WELL. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. AN ARBITRATOR'S FAILURE TO APPLY THE PRINCIPLES OF RES JUDICATA OR COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL PROPERLY IS NOT A BASIS TO VACATE AN ARBITRATION AWARD. (SEE DURAND V. WILSHIRE INS. CO. (1969) 270 CAL.APP.2D 58, 60; INTERINSURANCE EXCHANGE OF CA V. BAILES (1963) 219 CAL.APP.2D 830, 836.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
REMITTITUR DISMISSED (A133663 DIV 1)
NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STAY; APPENDIX, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY RESPONDENT JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION HEARING SET FOR OCT-20-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STAY; POS FILED BY PETITIONER FINISAR CORPORATION
APPENDIX OF NON-CAL AUTHORITIES IN SUPP OF OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENT'S NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO STAY; POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF FINISAR CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STAY IS DENIED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO DENY THE REQUESTED STAY ON THE FOLLOWING GROUNDS: THE CALIFORNIA ACTION WAS FILED FIRST AND IT SHOULD GO FORWARD DESPITE THE LATER FILED OUT OF STATE FEDERAL ACTION; AND THE MOTION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-20-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Forrest A. Hainline III, from Goodwin Procter LLP, 415-733-6000 for Petitioner Finisar Corporation
John V. Erickson, Esq., from Collette, Erickson, Farmer & O'Neill, LLP, 415-788-4646; Garrett J. Waltzer, Esq., and Claire A. McCormack, Esq., from Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP, 650-470-4540, for respondent JDSU

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. An arbitrator's failure to apply the principles of res judicata or collateral estoppel properly is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award. (See Durand v. Wilshire Ins. Co. (1969) 270 Cal.App.2d 58, 60; Interinsurance Exchange of CA v. Bailes (1963) 219 Cal.App.2d 830, 836.) Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay; Appendix
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John V. Erickson, Esq., from Collette, Erickson, Farmer & O'Neill, LLP, 415-788-4646; Garrett J. Waltzer, Esq., and Claire A. McCormack, Esq., from Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP, 650-470-4540, for respondent JDSU
Forrest A. Hainline III, from Goodwin Procter LLP, 415-733-6000 for Petitioner Finisar Corporation

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
RESPONDENT JDS UNIPHASE CORPORATION Motion To Stay is Denied. The Court exercises its discretion to deny the requested stay on the following grounds: the California action was filed first and it should go forward despite the later filed out of state federal action; and the motion to vacate the arbitration award is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-26-2011 Continued To Oct-20-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER FINISAR CORPORATION'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD.

APPEARANCES: Forrest A. Hainline, III, Esq. of Goodwin Procter 733-6065 for petitioner & moving party; Garrett J. Waltzer, Esq. of Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP 650/470-4540 for respondent & opposing party.

RULING: Petitioner provided a valid proof of service & in addition, respondent filed a "response" (opposition). Petitioner also indicated it filed an "opposition" to respondent's response. The Court continued the matter to 10/20/11 at 9:30am. Parties have provided courtesy copies. Respondent has also indicated that they have set a motion to stay for 10/20/11 as well. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
